claim: CONV-011: the tracker's 'settled' Set grows unboundedly for the tracker's life — every completed request id is retained forever to distinguish 'duplicate' from 'unknown-id', so a long-lived F16 connection (days, millions of requests) leaks memory on the hot path
fix: Ids are allocated monotonically from 1, so membership is derivable without any cache: id >= nextId => unknown-id; pending.has(id) => settled; otherwise (id < nextId, not pending) => duplicate. Drop the Set entirely.

claim: CONV-002/CONV-003: the maxFrameBytes override is never validated - passing NaN silently DISABLES the frame-size limit in BOTH directions (payload.byteLength > NaN and declared > NaN are both false), so a corrupt 4-byte prefix can demand a ~4 GiB allocation; 0/negative/fractional values likewise produce undocumented behavior instead of an actionable error
fix: Validate the override at encodeFrame/createFrameDecoder entry: require a positive safe integer <= 0xFFFFFFFF, else throw ProtocolError('bad-message'-class or a new config reason) naming the received value and the requirement.

claim: Diagnostic messages embed peer-controlled values UNBOUNDED and UNSANITIZED via JSON.stringify interpolation - a hostile frame can put multi-megabyte strings or raw C0/ESC bytes into error.message/detail, a terminal-escape-injection and log-flooding hazard the moment a consumer (F16 agent log, Termhalla main log) prints them
fix: Route every embedded offending value through one clamp+escape helper (e.g. first 120 chars, JSON-escape non-printables) before interpolation; keep full fidelity only in structured detail fields that are size-capped.

claim: Presence checks use the 'key in obj' operator, which is satisfied by INHERITED properties - a locally crafted object with a poisoned prototype (e.g. {type:'req',id:1,method:'m'} with params on its prototype) passes encodeFrame validation yet serializes WITHOUT the key, an accept-here/reject-there asymmetry; wire-decoded JSON objects are unaffected
fix: Use Object.hasOwn(obj, key) (or Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call) for every presence check so validation sees exactly what JSON.stringify will emit.

claim: The decoder grows its buffer by full-array concat on EVERY push and only compacts at frame boundaries - O(n^2) bytes copied for a large frame delivered in small chunks (an 8 MiB pty:data frame in 64 KiB chunks copies ~0.5 GB); this sits directly on the F17 flood/backpressure hot path the roadmap commits to proving
fix: Keep an array of pending chunks with a running total and join lazily only when a complete frame is available (or use a ring buffer); F17's flood scenario should benchmark it.

claim: Frozen-suite coverage gaps (CONV-002 boundary discipline): (a) no vector pins that a frame EXACTLY AT maxFrameBytes is accepted (only over-limit rejection is pinned - an off-by-one in the comparison would pass the suite); (b) no vector pins lone-surrogate round-trip for pty:data strings (a PTY chunk split mid-emoji yields a lone surrogate; ES2019 well-formed JSON.stringify handles it, but nothing pins that)
fix: Amend through a future tests-phase touch - F17's flood/flow-control suite is the natural site; propagate to the review-followups doc at doc-sync.

claim: createAgentHandshake performs no construction-time validation of its own advertisement - a JS caller passing an unknown/duplicate capability id only fails later at encodeFrame/peer-side parse, far from the mistake (CONV-001 spirit: surface the error at the input site)
fix: Validate init.capabilities in the factory (each isCapabilityId, no duplicates) and throw an actionable ProtocolError immediately.

claim: Design risk the spec and tests ignore: pty:data rides a JSON STRING inside the envelope, so flood-rate terminal output pays JSON escaping (2-6x expansion for control-heavy bytes) plus UTF-16<->UTF-8 transcoding per chunk on both ends; v1 has no binary frame variant and no benchmark evidencing the envelope survives the roadmap's committed flood scenario
fix: Treat F17's flood benchmark as the decision gate: if throughput or agent memory misses targets, reserve a length-prefixed BINARY payload frame type (protocol version is locked per-build, so adding one later is cheap); record in the followups doc.

claim: Codex cross-check (verified against the code by the orchestrating reviewer): parseWireMessage validates only the envelope, so non-JSON JS values in the any-JSON positions are ACCEPTED - {type:'req',id:1,method:'m',params:undefined} passes ('params' in obj is true for an own undefined) yet JSON.stringify OMITS the key, so encodeFrame emits bytes the decoder REJECTS (accept-here/reject-there on the core send path); a BigInt/cyclic payload makes encodeFrame throw a RAW TypeError instead of a structured ProtocolError; NaN/Infinity silently mutate to null. Contradicts REQ-007's MUST ('{ok:false} for everything else' - undefined is not 'any JSON value'), REQ-003's MUST (structured bad-message on malformed; encode/decode symmetry), and REQ-015's MUST (every surfaced error carries the closed-union reason). Wire-decoded input is unaffected (JSON.parse cannot produce these values) - the defect is local-API-domain only, but F16's transport types params as unknown, so a natural undefined slips through silently and fails remotely.
fix: Add deep JSON-representability validation for req.params, res(ok:true).result, and each evt args entry inside parseWireMessage (reject undefined, functions, symbols, bigint, non-finite numbers, non-plain objects incl. toJSON carriers, and cycles, each with a structured bad-message naming the path) - zero wire-behavior change, frozen suite verified compatible. Codex rated HIGH; contract_violation set per reviewer doctrine (genuine spec MUST contradiction).
resolution: Verified fixed by the filing lens (codex cross-check re-verification, VERIFIED-FIXED) + orchestrating-reviewer probes (18/18): validateJsonRepresentable (messages.ts) deep-checks the three any-JSON positions (req.params / res(ok:true).result / evt.args) - undefined/function/symbol/bigint rejected naming the path; NaN/Infinity rejected (no silent null); cycles rejected structurally (encodeFrame now throws ProtocolError bad-message, never a raw TypeError - probe 5f); non-plain objects rejected naming the bounded constructor; toJSON carriers rejected via a [[Get]] lookup mirroring JSON.stringify's own semantics on BOTH arrays and plain objects (incl. the inherited-toJSON array repro Object.setPrototypeOf([1],{toJSON:()=>5}) from the NOT-FIXED round); own non-index array expandos rejected naming the key. Zero wire-behavior change verified: plain JSON/DAGs/null-proto objects accepted, 200k-deep frames accepted with no RangeError (iterative walk), round-trip intact, frozen suite green, implement gate re-derived green. Landed via ESC-001 option (a): A/B round (winner codex:codex-rescue) + inline completion patch (orky:implementer at tier high after the codex winner-stays dispatch stalled on stdin and was killed at the 600s bound).

claim: Re-review of the FINDING-009 fix (codex cross-check, empirically confirmed by the orchestrating reviewer): LOCAL hostile inputs with throwing accessors/Proxy traps escape validateJsonRepresentable as RAW JS errors instead of a structured bad-message - Object.getPrototypeOf, the toJSON own-property probe, Object.keys, and child property reads all invoke traps/getters ([[Get]]), so parseWireMessage({...params:{get x(){throw}}}) THROWS raw (probe-verified: 'Error: gotcha-getter') where its contract is to RETURN {ok:false}. Wire-decoded input is unaffected (JSON.parse output carries no getters/proxies).
fix: Wrap the representability walk so trap-thrown exceptions convert to a structured bad-message naming the best-known path and a bounded cause; zero wire-behavior change.
resolution: Verified fixed at the filed scope (probe: parseWireMessage({params:{get x(){throw}}}) now RETURNS a structured bad-message 'trap-throw' with best-known path + bounded cause instead of raw-throwing): the whole validateJsonRepresentable walk is wrapped, converting accessor/Proxy-trap exceptions from prototype lookups, toJSON [[Get]]s, Object.keys, and child reads. Residual OUTSIDE this finding's scope (codex re-verify note, pre-existing since the original envelope validator): top-level envelope reads (m.type/m.params/Object.keys(m)/JSON.stringify diagnostics in the old bad() call sites) can still raw-throw if the FRAME ITSELF is a hostile local Proxy - unreachable from wire input and from ordinary local construction; routed to the review-followups doc at doc-sync alongside FINDING-003/004 local-domain hardening.

claim: The new non-plain-object rejection in validateJsonRepresentable reports only 'got object' (describeJsonType collapses every object to typeof) - a Date, Map, Uint8Array, and arbitrary class instance are indistinguishable in both message and detail, weakening CONV-001/REQ-015 actionability exactly where the caller most needs to know WHICH wrapper type leaked into a params/result/args position.
fix: Name the constructor/prototype (bounded, e.g. slice(0,60)) in the message and a detail field for the non-plain-object branch; never interpolate the value itself.
resolution: Verified fixed (probe: params: new Uint8Array(1) now rejects with 'non-plain object (Uint8Array)' - the constructor named in BOTH message and detail.prototype, bounded slice(0,60), 'null-prototype' fallback; the value itself never interpolated). Check order also improved: the non-plain rejection now precedes the toJSON check, so a Date is named as Date rather than generically as a toJSON carrier.

claim: Residual silent-narrowing in the FINDING-009 divergence family (probe-verified): an ARRAY with own non-index enumerable properties (Object.assign([1],{foo:2})) passes validateJsonRepresentable (the array branch walks indices only) yet JSON.stringify silently DROPS the expando, so the validated frame and the peer-decoded frame differ (foo vanishes). Wire-decoded input unaffected (JSON.parse never produces expando arrays). Weaker observable than the toJSON residual (no crash, no reject - silent content narrowing), but the same validate-here/serialize-differently class.
fix: In the array branch, reject own enumerable non-index keys naming the key (JSON.stringify ignores them), alongside the inherited-toJSON closure.
resolution: Verified fixed (probe: Object.assign([1],{foo:2}) in params now rejects with 'array-expando' naming key foo; codex re-verification lists own non-index array expandos in the covered reject set): the array branch validates every own enumerable key as a canonical index (String(Number(key))===key, integer, 0<=idx<length) before walking entries, so keys JSON.stringify would silently drop are rejected at the input site. Canonical-form edge keys ('01', '-0', '1e2', out-of-range numerics) all reject; real indices and holes behave as before (holes still reject as undefined entries).
